description: The ValueMultiplexer component allows one to curate a list of values and drive a target with one of them, much like a multiplexer.

fields.Target: A field which is driven with the currently selected list value
fields.Index: 0-based index that determines which value of ``Values`` has been selected; values outside the range ``[0;length-1]`` are wrapped around internally.
fields.Values: A list of values which can also individually be driven or written to
fields.AllowWriteBack: Setting this to ``true`` redirects writes from ``Target`` or from a ``Source`` representing the selected value to the currently indexed list entry.

usage:
The component behaves similar to a ValueCopy with the list entry indicated by ``Index`` as the value to output. Changes to ``Index`` or the list entries will affect ``Target`` whenever the drive is evaluated.

In addition, the component implements the IValue interface. This allows the component to be sourced by dragging out the component header with the ProtoFlux tip. This allows one to use the component like an array when sourcing the ``Index`` field as well. The source of this component will update instantly, as opposed to the drive being limited to once per frame.
